stateOfAdventism.title: State of Adventism 2024
stateOfAdventism.subtitle: The annual global health report for the Seventh-day Adventist Church. 22.5 million members, 13 divisions, one mission.

briefs.title: The $41,900 Question
briefs.subtitle: What it actually costs to baptise one person in some Australian conferences

briefs.title: The 40% You Don't See
briefs.subtitle: Australian tithe-to-income ratios have dropped 40% in 20 years — while absolute tithe keeps rising

briefs.title: The Pareto Pew
briefs.subtitle: In most churches, 20% of givers fund 80% of the budget. Are Adventist churches any different?

briefs.title: The Missing Millions
briefs.subtitle: 47 million people have been Adventist since 1965. Only 22.8 million still are.

briefs.title: The Phantom Church
briefs.subtitle: 60% of Adventists on the books aren't in the building on Sabbath morning

briefs.title: The 98% Pipeline
briefs.subtitle: 98.2% of youth who complete K-12 Adventist education stay in the church. Only 27% without it do.

briefs.title: They Didn't Leave Because of Doctrine
briefs.subtitle: The #1 reason former Adventists cite for leaving: perceived hypocrisy — not theological disagreement

briefs.title: The Gen Z Plot Twist
briefs.subtitle: Gen Z is attending church more than any other generation. But there's a catch.

briefs.title: The 2.7-Year Problem
briefs.subtitle: Adventist pastors move every 2.7 years. Peak effectiveness starts at year 5.

briefs.title: Running on Empty
briefs.subtitle: 44% of Adventist pastors feel 'run down and drained.' In Asia, 75% report severe stress.

briefs.title: More Administrators Than Pastors
briefs.subtitle: The NAD has 59 conferences — and more people in offices than in pulpits

briefs.title: The Generational Giving Cliff
briefs.subtitle: Average church donations dropped 9.2% in real terms. The top givers are aging out.

briefs.title: The 17-23 Window
briefs.subtitle: 40-50% of baptised Adventist teens leave by their mid-20s. But the crisis starts earlier than you think.

briefs.title: Are We Growing — Or Just Importing?
briefs.subtitle: How much of Western Adventist growth comes from migration vs. converting locals?

briefs.title: The Back Door Is Bigger Than the Front Door
briefs.subtitle: In Inter-America, 87% of gains are offset by losses. The church is churning, not growing.

briefs.title: Only Half Are Vegetarian
briefs.subtitle: 47% of Adventist Health Study participants eat meat regularly. The health message is more diverse than you think.

briefs.title: The Sabbath Effect
briefs.subtitle: Emerging research links weekly Sabbath rest to reduced anxiety, less burnout, and greater life satisfaction

briefs.title: The Grey Pulpit
briefs.subtitle: Average NAD pastor age: 55. Half will retire in 10 years. The pipeline can't keep up.

briefs.title: One Church Every Three Hours
briefs.subtitle: The Adventist Church plants 8 churches per day globally. But only 4% of NAD churches have ever reproduced.

briefs.title: The Entering Wedge — Or the Stumbling Block?
briefs.subtitle: The health message opens doors when presented as invitation. It slams them shut when presented as law.

briefs.title: The Salary Nobody Talks About
briefs.subtitle: Adventist pastoral salaries range from $5,000 to $85,000 depending on where you serve. Same credentials, different continents.

briefs.title: The Deceleration Nobody Expected
briefs.subtitle: Global South growth is the Adventist success story. But the growth rate is slowing.

briefs.title: The $255,000 Question
briefs.subtitle: The estimated cost to retain one additional member through K-12 Adventist education: a quarter million dollars

briefs.title: The Teacher Exodus
briefs.subtitle: 30% of departing Adventist teachers are replaced by non-Adventists. Is the education system's mission drifting?

briefs.title: Who Keeps Their Kids?
briefs.subtitle: Adventists retain about 50-65% of youth. Mormons: 64%. Hindus: 82%. What's the difference?

briefs.title: Pitcairn Island: 96% Adventist
briefs.subtitle: The countries with the highest Adventist ratios are mostly places you've never heard of

briefs.title: The $6 Million Meeting
briefs.subtitle: GC Sessions cost ~$8-10 million per quinquennium. The cascade through all levels may reach $78 million.

briefs.title: Trust the Numbers, Question the Framing
briefs.subtitle: A guide to the Adventist media landscape — and why every outlet has blind spots

briefs.title: Show Me the Books
briefs.subtitle: The Adventist Church audits itself. Is that enough?

briefs.title: The 15% Sabbath
briefs.subtitle: In North America, possibly fewer than 1 in 5 Adventist members attend church on a typical Sabbath
